title: Bread Dough Types
content:
TYPES OF DOUGH

Batters: Simplest; require little handling. Beat to develop gluten. More porous, dry out faster than kneaded breads.

Sponge Doughs: Yeast dissolved in larger amount of water; portion of flour added. Ferment until foamy (up to 1 hour); then add butter, eggs, salt, sugar, remaining flour. Lighter, coarser-grained than straight dough.

Straight/Conventional Dough: Dissolve yeast in warm liquid (85°F compressed, 105-115°F dry) with small amount of sugar 5-10 minutes. Add flour gradually, beating after first half. Turn onto floured board when dough leaves sides of bowl. Rest 10-15 minutes covered, then knead.

Mixer Method: Blend active dry yeast with part of flour and dry ingredients. Heat liquids and shortening to 120-130°F; add to flour-yeast mixture. Beat 2 minutes medium, add eggs and additional cup flour, beat ½ minute low, then 3 minutes high. Stir in remaining ingredients and enough flour for soft dough; proceed as for straight dough.
